rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Embed exact embedded-packet field paths in a dedicated 'source' column for every input and derived numeric cell (use format: metadata_json.embedded_packet.sections.S2.arc_count_per_pass). Ensure the spreadsheet is produced in a standard, parseable format (so openpyxl or equivalent can validate formulas). rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Both required artifacts are present (deterministic precheck passed). The memo contains clear situation description, numeric calculations (e.g., multiplicative 45% per 40 km/h step applied to 17 arcs -> 9.35 and 5.14), sensitivity testing, explicit decision labels (fail / immediate mitigation / maintenance / verification), and cost numbers for actions. The workbook could not be programmatically parsed in this environment (openpyxl unavailable) so row-level cell-level verification of formulas and embedded-packet explicit path citations could not be confirmed. The memo explicitly states it used embedded-packet assumptions but does not include the exact expected row-level citation format (metadata_json.embedded_packet.sections.S2.arc_count_per_pass) for every numeric derivation. Given the observable memo content and the reported workbook generation steps (builder created OOXML with formulas), most major rubric requirements appear satisfied; the missing, unverifiable explicit row-level citation evidence (and inability to parse the xlsx to confirm spreadsheet formulas/source columns) prevents awarding full credit.
